attractions.name: Automobile Alley Historic District
attractions.description: Automobile Alley is a vibrant historic district in Oklahoma City known for its upscale urban atmosphere, neon-lit buildings, and rich automotive history. Once home to over 50 car dealerships in the 1920s, the area has been revitalized into a premier cultural hub featuring top restaurants, retail shops, and immersive art experiences. Automobile Alley, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: N Broadway Ave (from NW 4th St to NW 13th St)
attractions.hours: District accessible 24/7; Business hours typically Mon-Sat 10:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Sun 12:00 PM - 6:00 PM (Dining/Bars open later).
attractions.days_open: Daily
attractions.admission: Free (Public District); individual attractions like Factory Obscura or museums have separate fees.
highlights: Iconic historic neon signs of former car dealerships that light up the Broadway corridor at night.
Factory Obscura's Mix-Tape, a 6,000-square-foot immersive and interactive art experience.
The Oklahoma Contemporary Arts Center, which hosts rotating modern art exhibitions in a striking architectural space.
A diverse culinary scene featuring Broadway 10 Bar & Chophouse, Red PrimeSteak, and local favorites like S&B's Burger Joint.
Unique local shopping at boutiques such as Plenty Mercantile, Shop Good, and Common Dear.
The annual 'Lights on Broadway' holiday event featuring over 180,000 colorful neon lights.
attractions.tips: Visit at night to fully experience the district's signature vintage neon signs. Use the OKC Streetcar for easy navigation between Automobile Alley and other downtown districts. Keep an eye out for the 'Alley Art Project' which features colorful murals on various doors throughout the neighborhood. Visit OKC
attractions.nearby: Midtown, Oklahoma City National Memorial & Museum, Bricktown, Scissortail Park

attractions.name: Boathouse District (RIVERSPORT OKC)
attractions.description: The Boathouse District is a world-class destination for athletes and families located on the Oklahoma River in downtown Oklahoma City. It features iconic modern architecture, a U.S. Olympic & Paralympic Training Site, and the RIVERSPORT Adventure Park with whitewater rafting, zip lining, and high-speed slides. Riversport OKC, Visit OKC
attractions.address: 800 RIVERSPORT Drive
attractions.hours: RIVERSPORT Adventures & Rafting: Sun-Fri 10am-5pm, Sat 10am-6pm (Daily starting Memorial Day); NightWater: Thu-Fri 5pm-8pm (Seasonal); Trailhead Bike Park: 6am-10pm daily; Boathouse Fitness Centers: Mon-Fri 12pm-8pm, Sat-Sun 12pm-5pm. Riversport OKC
attractions.days_open: Open daily starting Memorial Day through early August; Open weekends in May and late August through September. Riversport OKC
attractions.admission: RIVERSPORT Day Pass: $65; Adventure Day Pass: $45; NightWater Pass: $25; Season Membership: $125; Boathouse District Playground: Free. Riversport OKC
highlights: RIVERSPORT Rapids whitewater rafting and kayaking center
SandRidge Sky Trail, an 80-foot adventure course and world's tallest of its kind
SandRidge Sky Zip, a 700-foot zip line across the Oklahoma River
Official U.S. Olympic and Paralympic Training Site for rowing and canoe/kayak
Surf OKC standing wave surfing experience
Over 13 miles of paved trails for running and cycling
attractions.tips: Secure footwear (no flip-flops, slides, or Crocs) is required for all adventure activities. It is recommended to book passes online in advance to guarantee availability for rafting and zip-lining. Riversport OKC, Airial Travel
attractions.nearby: Bricktown Entertainment District, Centennial Land Run Monument, Scissortail Park, Chickasaw Bricktown Ballpark, Myriad Botanical Gardens. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Bricktown Entertainment District
attractions.description: Bricktown is Oklahoma City's premier entertainment destination, a former warehouse district transformed into a vibrant urban hub. It features a scenic mile-long canal, historic brick buildings housing over 45 restaurants and bars, and major attractions like the Chickasaw Bricktown Ballpark. Visit OKC, Bricktown OKC
attractions.address: E. Sheridan Ave. & S. Mickey Mantle Dr.
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: Open 24 hours (Business hours vary) Visit OKC
attractions.days_open: Daily Bricktown OKC
attractions.admission: Free (Individual attractions like the Water Taxi or museums have separate fees) Visit OKC
highlights: Scenic Bricktown Canal with guided Water Taxi tours
Chickasaw Bricktown Ballpark, home of the OKC Comets baseball team
Centennial Land Run Monument featuring 45 life-sized bronze figures
Vibrant nightlife and over 45 diverse dining and retail options
American Banjo Museum and Oklahoma Sports Hall of Fame
attractions.tips: Be aware of the 9:00 PM curfew for unaccompanied minors. Use the Oklahoma City Streetcar for easy access between downtown districts. Parking is available in several garages and lots throughout the district. Bricktown OKC
attractions.nearby: Paycom Center, Scissortail Park, Myriad Botanical Gardens, Oklahoma City National Memorial & Museum Wikipedia

attractions.name: Deep Deuce District
attractions.description: Deep Deuce is a historic urban neighborhood in downtown Oklahoma City that served as the heart of the city's African American community and a national hub for jazz music during the 1920s and 1930s. Today, it has undergone a vibrant renaissance, transforming into a walkable residential and entertainment district filled with modern apartments, art galleries, and acclaimed restaurants. Deep Deuce OKC, Visit OKC
attractions.address: Centered on NE 2nd St (roughly between Main St and NE 4th St, Broadway to I-235)
attractions.hours: Generally accessible 24/7 (Public District); Individual business hours vary Deep Deuce OKC
attractions.days_open: Daily Deep Deuce OKC
attractions.admission: Free Deep Deuce OKC
highlights: Birthplace and home of 'Invisible Man' author Ralph Ellison
Former hub for legendary jazz musicians like Charlie Christian and Jimmy Rushing
Home to Artspace at Untitled and various contemporary art galleries
Vibrant dining scene featuring the historic Deep Deuce Grill and modern eateries
Walkable, pet-friendly atmosphere with the dedicated Deep Deuce Dog Park
Preserved historical significance as a center for Black culture and commerce
attractions.tips: Visit the Deep Deuce Grill for its popular patio and firepit. Take a walking tour to see historical markers and plaques detailing the district's jazz heritage. Use the nearby Bricktown Water Taxi for a scenic connection to other downtown areas. Amanda's OK, Deep Deuce OKC
attractions.nearby: Bricktown Entertainment District, Oklahoma City National Memorial & Museum, Chickasaw Bricktown Ballpark, Myriad Botanical Gardens Visit OKC

attractions.name: Film Row District (Film Exchange Historic District)
attractions.description: Film Row is a historic 42-block district that served as a major film distribution hub for Hollywood studios like Paramount, MGM, and Warner Bros. in the 1930s. Today, it is a revitalized center for creativity and nightlife, featuring preserved Art Deco architecture, film-oriented businesses, and popular dining and entertainment venues. Wikipedia, Downtown OKC
attractions.address: W. Sheridan Ave. (between Walker Ave. and Classen Blvd.)
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (District access); individual business hours vary Downtown OKC
attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday Downtown OKC
attractions.admission: Free Visit OKC, West Village OKC
highlights: Historic Art Deco and Art Moderne architecture formerly housing film exchanges. West Village OKC
The Jones Assembly, a popular restaurant, bar, and live music venue. Visit OKC
Sailor & The Dock, a creative shipping container market and event space. Sailor & The Dock
Flashback RetroPub, featuring over 100 classic arcade games and retro themed drinks. Visit OKC
The Oklahoma Film Exchange, which preserves the district's heritage with historic screenings. The Journal Record
attractions.tips: Look for the original film vaults and screening rooms still present in many of the historic buildings. Visit during the second Friday of the month for special events at Sailor & The Dock, and don't miss the unique 'Killer Queen' arcade game at Flashback RetroPub. Visit OKC, Sailor & The Dock
attractions.nearby: Scissortail Park, Myriad Botanical Gardens, Oklahoma City Museum of Art, Civic Center Music Hall Visit OKC, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Inasmuch Foundation Crystal Bridge Conservatory
attractions.description: The Inasmuch Foundation Crystal Bridge Conservatory is a stunning 224-foot-long cylindrical glass greenhouse that serves as the centerpiece of the Myriad Botanical Gardens. Spanning 13,000 square feet, it features two distinct climates—a tropical wet zone and a tropical dry zone—housing a diverse collection of exotic plants and immersive educational exhibits (Myriad Botanical Gardens). The structure's unique architectural design consists of 3,028 translucent acrylic panels and was recently reimagined through a significant renovation (Metro Family Magazine).
attractions.address: 301 W. Reno
attractions.hours: Monday through Saturday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Sunday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Myriad Botanical Gardens)
attractions.days_open: Open daily except Thanksgiving Day and Christmas Day (Myriad Botanical Gardens)
attractions.admission: Adult: $10.50, Senior (62+ years): $9, Military/Veterans: $9, Student: $7, Youth (5-17 years): $5.50, Ages 4 & under: FREE, Myriad Gardens Members: FREE (Myriad Botanical Gardens)
highlights: 224-foot long cylindrical glass conservatory with 3,028 acrylic panels (Myriad Botanical Gardens)
Two distinct climates: a tropical wet zone and a tropical dry zone at the north end (Myriad Botanical Gardens)
Sensory Skywalk on the third level offering interactive displays on sounds, tastes, and smells (Myriad Botanical Gardens)
Newly redesigned two-level cascading waterfall feature with tropical water plants (Myriad Botanical Gardens)
Root Wall and reflecting pool at the north end featuring exotic water lilies (Myriad Botanical Gardens)
Cloud Portal Sculpture acting as a vertical gateway between the north and south ends (Myriad Botanical Gardens)
attractions.tips: Visit the second-floor Discovery Room for family-friendly interactive activities, and explore the Oculus Gallery for seasonal views of the gardens (Myriad Botanical Gardens). While only service animals are allowed inside the conservatory, leashed pets are welcome on the exterior grounds which also feature a dedicated dog park (Myriad Botanical Gardens).
attractions.nearby: Scissortail Park, Paycom Center, Oklahoma City National Memorial & Museum, Bricktown Entertainment District, Devon Energy Center (Wikipedia, VisitOKC)

Founded in 1979 by poets, it serves as a community hub for thought-provoking exhibitions and educational programs. Uncovering Oklahoma, Oklahoma City Art Guide

attractions.admission: Free (Nearly 100% of programs and gallery admission are free to the public) Uncovering Oklahoma

"Biting the Apple" - An annual erotic art show and primary fundraiser Oklahoma Gazette
Experimental poetry and performance art events Uncovering Oklahoma
Educational programs and community art partnerships Dennis Spielman
Commitment to keeping exhibitions accessible and free to the public Uncovering Oklahoma
attractions.tips: Visit during a lunch break to enjoy current exhibits in a quiet environment. Check the annual calendar for the "Biting the Apple" event for a unique, themed experience. Uncovering Oklahoma, Oklahoma Gazette

attractions.name: Love's Travel Stops Stage and Great Lawn (Scissortail Park Official Site)
attractions.description: The Love's Travel Stops Stage and Great Lawn serves as the centerpiece of Scissortail Park's northern section, functioning as a premier outdoor performance venue for the community. The six-acre space features a 54,000-square-foot reinforced lawn and a modern covered stage designed to host everything from intimate receptions to large-scale concerts for up to 25,000 guests. It offers a scenic environment with the Oklahoma City skyline as a backdrop, making it a popular hub for movies, symphonic performances, and festivals (Official Site Description, Visit OKC).
attractions.address: 300 SW 7th St, Oklahoma City, OK 73109 (Scissortail Park FAQ, Visit OKC)
attractions.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM; Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM; Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM; Thursday: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM; Friday: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM; Saturday: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM; Sunday: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM (Scissortail Park Official Site)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Scissortail Park Official Site)
attractions.admission: Free for the general public, though certain performances or festivals may require separate tickets (Scissortail Park FAQ)
highlights: Hosts a variety of free concerts and movie screenings throughout the year (Visit OKC)
Large 54,000-square-foot reinforced lawn area for comfortable seating (Official Site Venue Details)
Modern performance stage with professional lighting, sound, and VIP green room (Official Site Venue Details)
Stunning views of the Oklahoma City skyline and nearby Devon Tower (Yelp Review Summary)
Capacity for major events hosting up to 25,000 standing guests (Official Site Venue Details)
Central location within the Upper Park, adjacent to the Promenade and ornamental gardens (Official Site Maps)
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to bring blankets or low-profile lawn chairs for event seating. Check the official park calendar before visiting, as the lawn is occasionally closed for 'rest periods' to maintain the turf quality. Metered parking is available along the park's perimeter, but can fill up quickly during major concert events (Visit OKC Tips, Official Site Venue Notes).
attractions.nearby: Myriad Botanical Gardens, Omni Oklahoma City Hotel, Oklahoma City Convention Center, Paycom Center, Skydance Bridge (Official Site News, Visit OKC)

attractions.name: Myriad Botanical Gardens
attractions.description: Myriad Botanical Gardens is a 15-acre natural escape in the heart of downtown Oklahoma City, featuring lush ornamental gardens and diverse landscapes. The centerpiece is the Inasmuch Foundation Crystal Bridge Conservatory, a 224-foot architectural marvel housing thousands of tropical and desert plants. Visitors can enjoy a variety of outdoor amenities including a children's garden, dog park, and seasonal events like movies on the lawn. (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 301 W. Reno Ave, Oklahoma City, OK 73102
attractions.hours: Crystal Bridge Conservatory & Gift Shop: Mon-Sat 9am-5pm, Sun 11am-5pm. Outdoor Grounds: Daily 6am-11pm. Mo's Carousel: Daily 9am-8pm. (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Outdoor grounds open daily; Conservatory and Gift Shop open daily except Thanksgiving and Christmas. (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Outdoor Grounds: Free. Crystal Bridge Conservatory: Adult $10.50, Senior (62+) $9, Military/Veterans $9, Student $7, Youth (5-17) $5.50, Ages 4 & under Free, Members Free. (Official Website)
highlights: Inasmuch Foundation Crystal Bridge Conservatory
Children’s Garden and Playground
Myriad Gardens Dog Park
Meinders Terrace and Great Lawn
Mo's Carousel
Seasonal Devon Ice Rink
attractions.tips: Visit early in the morning for cooler temperatures and fewer crowds. Wear comfortable walking shoes to explore the 15-acre grounds. Check the event calendar for seasonal displays and movies on the lawn. Be prepared for high humidity inside the Crystal Bridge Conservatory. (Tripadvisor, Official Website)
attractions.nearby: Scissortail Park, Paycom Center, Oklahoma City Museum of Art, Oklahoma City National Memorial & Museum, Bricktown District. (Wikipedia, Visit OKC)

attractions.name: Oklahoma City National Memorial & Museum (Survivor Tree)
attractions.description: The Survivor Tree is a nearly 100-year-old American elm that survived the 1995 bombing of the Alfred P. Murrah Federal Building despite being located just yards from the blast. Once an unassuming tree in a parking lot, it was saved from being cut down for evidence and now stands at the highest point of the memorial as a profound symbol of resilience and hope for the city. Official Website
attractions.address: 620 N Harvey Ave, Oklahoma City, OK 73102
attractions.hours: Outdoor Symbolic Memorial: Open 24 hours a day. Museum: Monday–Saturday 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Sunday 12:00 PM – 5:00 PM. Official Website
attractions.days_open: Outdoor Memorial: 365 days a year. Museum: Open daily (Closed Easter, Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Eve, Christmas Day, and New Year’s Day). Official Website
attractions.admission: Outdoor Symbolic Memorial: Free. Museum: Adults $18, Seniors (62+) $16, Military (with ID) $16, Students (ages 6–17 or college) $15, Children (5 and under) Free. Official Website
highlights: The Survivor Tree: A nearly 100-year-old American elm and central symbol of resilience.
The Gates of Time: Two massive bronze gates marking the minutes before and after the blast (9:01 and 9:03).
The Field of Empty Chairs: 168 hand-crafted bronze and stone chairs representing each person killed.
The Reflecting Pool: A thin layer of water flowing over black granite providing a quiet space for reflection.
Rescuers' Orchard: A grove of trees surrounding the Survivor Tree to honor those who came to help.
The Survivor Wall: Original granite panels from the Murrah Building etched with the names of over 600 survivors. National Park Service
attractions.tips: Visit the outdoor memorial at night to see the Field of Empty Chairs illuminated from below. Download the 'Stronger Together' AR app for an enhanced storytelling experience. Plan for about 1.5 to 2 hours if visiting both the museum and the grounds. Official Website
attractions.nearby: Oklahoma City Museum of Art, Myriad Botanical Gardens, Bricktown Entertainment District, Scissortail Park. Visit OKC

attractions.name: Paycom Center
attractions.description: Paycom Center is a premier multi-purpose arena located in the heart of downtown Oklahoma City. It serves as the home venue for the NBA's Oklahoma City Thunder and hosts a wide variety of world-class concerts, family shows, and sporting events throughout the year. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 100 W. Reno Ave., Oklahoma City, OK 73102
attractions.hours: Administrative Office: Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Box Office: Tuesday & Thursday 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM (also opens 4 hours prior to events on event days). Contact Us, Ticket Information
attractions.days_open: Open daily for scheduled events; Administrative office open Monday - Friday. Contact Us
attractions.admission: Prices vary by event; tickets are available through official providers Ticketmaster and AXS. The arena is a cashless facility. Ticket Information, Ticketmaster
highlights: Home of the NBA's Oklahoma City Thunder and NBA G League's Oklahoma City Blue.
Hosts major concert tours, professional wrestling, and family ice shows.
Features premium seating options including 3,380 club seats and 49 private suites.
Operated by Legends Global and owned by the City of Oklahoma City.
Conveniently served by the Oklahoma City Streetcar at the Arena station.
State-of-the-art facility with over 581,000 square feet of space.
attractions.tips: The arena is a strictly cashless facility, accepting only credit and debit cards for all purchases. To avoid additional service fees, consider purchasing tickets directly at the box office during its Tuesday and Thursday operating hours. Be aware of the strict bag policy which generally prohibits bags larger than 4.5\" x 6.5\" unless they are clear. FAQs, Ticket Information
attractions.nearby: Scissortail Park, Myriad Botanical Gardens, Oklahoma City Convention Center, Chickasaw Bricktown Ballpark. Visit OKC

attractions.name: Bricktown Brewery (Official Website)
attractions.description: Established in 1992, Bricktown Brewery was one of the first businesses to open in Oklahoma City's historic warehouse district. This flagship location serves award-winning craft beers brewed on-site alongside a menu of elevated comfort food in a lively, converted warehouse setting. It has since expanded to numerous locations across multiple states while maintaining its roots as a local favorite in the heart of Bricktown (Official Website, Ride OKC).
attractions.address: 1 N Oklahoma Ave, Oklahoma City, OK 73104 (Official Website)
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Friday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM; Saturday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM; Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM (Official Website, Tripadvisor)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website)
highlights: Flagship location and birthplace of the Bricktown Brewery brand, established in 1992 (Ride OKC).
In-house brewing operations visible through a glass-enclosed room overlooking the main bar (Ride OKC).
Signature award-winning craft beers, including the Bluesberry Ale and Six String Stout (Ride OKC).
Menu featuring local Oklahoma favorites like beer-battered Watonga cheddar curds (Official Website).
Historic warehouse setting situated in the heart of the Bricktown entertainment district (Visit OKC).
Offers private event spaces for parties and corporate gatherings (Official Website).
attractions.tips: A popular spot for pre-game and post-game dining due to its proximity to the Paycom Center and ballpark. Be sure to try the Watonga cheese curds, a highly recommended local favorite. The restaurant is family-friendly and offers large party platters for groups (Ride OKC, Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Chickasaw Bricktown Ballpark, American Banjo Museum, Bricktown Canal Water Taxi, Paycom Center, Oklahoma Sports Hall of Fame (Bricktown OKC, Visit OKC)

attractions.description: The Bricktown Canal is a central waterway and major tourist destination in Oklahoma City's historic warehouse district. It features scenic pedestrian walkways, narrated boat tours, and is lined with a diverse array of restaurants, shops, and public art installations, including the massive Centennial Land Run Monument. Wikipedia, Airial Travel

attractions.hours: Canal Walkways: Open 24/7. Water Taxi Operating Hours: Monday 10:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Tuesday 10:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Wednesday 10:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Thursday 10:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Friday 10:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Saturday 10:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Sunday 10:00 AM - 10:00 PM. Tripadvisor

highlights: Narrated boat tours via the Bricktown Water Taxi Bricktown Water Taxi
Centennial Land Run Monument at the east end of the canal Airial Travel
Scenic canal-side walkways with bridges and colorful murals Wikipedia
Brickopolis Entertainment center featuring mini-golf and laser tag Airial Travel
Numerous restaurants with outdoor patio seating overlooking the water Welcome to Bricktown

attractions.name: Chickasaw Bricktown Ballpark
attractions.description: Chickasaw Bricktown Ballpark is a premier Triple-A baseball stadium and a cornerstone of Oklahoma City's vibrant Bricktown Entertainment District. Opened in 1998, the stadium features a modern retro-style design with 480,000 bricks and serves as the home field for the Oklahoma City Comets, the Triple-A affiliate of the Los Angeles Dodgers. (Wikipedia, MiLB.com)
attractions.address: 2 S Mickey Mantle Dr, Oklahoma City, OK 73104-2433
attractions.hours: Ticket Office (Game Days): Mon–Thu: 4 PM - Final Out; Fri–Sat: 2 PM - Final Out; Sun: 2 hours before pitch - Final Out. Ticket Office (Non-Game Days): Mon–Fri: 9 AM - 6 PM. Team Store: Mon–Fri: 10 AM - 5 PM; Sat (Game Days): 10 AM - 5 PM. (MiLB.com, Ticketmaster)
attractions.days_open: Open daily for games during the season (April–September); Team Store and Ticket Office open Monday–Friday year-round (excluding holidays). (MiLB.com)
attractions.admission: Starts at $9 for single-game tickets; prices vary by seat section, opponent, and event. (Visit Oklahoma City)
highlights: Home of the Oklahoma City Comets, the Triple-A affiliate of the Los Angeles Dodgers. (MiLB.com)
Life-sized bronze statues of baseball legends Mickey Mantle, Johnny Bench, and Warren Spahn. (Wikipedia)
Classic architecture featuring over 480,000 bricks in the historic warehouse district. (Visit Oklahoma City)
Hosts major events including the Phillips 66 Big 12 Baseball Tournament and the Bedlam Series. (Wikipedia)
Features 30 entertainment suites and the Oklahoma Fidelity Bank Club for premium viewing experiences. (MiLB.com)
Located adjacent to the iconic Flaming Lips Alley and the Bricktown Canal. (MiLB.com)
attractions.tips: Sit on the 3rd base side for the best shade during afternoon and evening games. Consider purchasing 'Comets Cash' to save on concessions and merchandise, and book parking at the Joe Carter Lot in advance for the easiest access. (Tripadvisor, MiLB.com)
attractions.nearby: Bricktown Canal, Bricktown Water Taxi, Centennial Land Run Monument, Brickopolis Entertainment, Sonic Plaza, and many restaurants and bars within the Bricktown District. (Visit Oklahoma City)

Formally dedicated in 2007 following a worldwide fan campaign, the alley serves as a whimsical tribute to the band's local roots and their influence on the city's culture. (Wikipedia, Atlas Obscura)
attractions.address: 124 E Sheridan Ave (and surrounding alleyway)
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Wikipedia)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Wikipedia)
attractions.admission: Free (Atlas Obscura)
highlights: Named after the Oklahoma City-based psychedelic rock band The Flaming Lips (Wikipedia)
Officially dedicated on October 25, 2007, by Mayor Mick Cornett (Atlas Obscura)
Located directly behind the Chickasaw Bricktown Ballpark outfield (MLB.com)
Features distinctive street signage that has become a popular photo destination (Visit Oklahoma City)
Symbolizes the city's transformation and its support for local arts and music (The Journal Record)
attractions.tips: Visit for a unique photo opportunity with the street signs, and explore the nearby canal and ballpark for the full Bricktown experience. The alley is a public thoroughfare and can be visited at any time, but it is especially vibrant during baseball season. (Visit Oklahoma City)
attractions.nearby: Chickasaw Bricktown Ballpark, Oklahoma Sports Hall of Fame, American Banjo Museum, Bricktown Canal (Wikipedia, Atlas Obscura)

Established in 2008 following a relocation from Seattle, the team has become a central fixture of Oklahoma City's culture, known for its passionate "Loud City" fan base (Wikipedia).

Dedicated Five-Course and Vegan Four-Course tasting menus for refined dining. Vast Dinner
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended. The attire is business casual to formal; avoid overly casual wear. Valet parking is available for dinner, while the Devon Garage is suggested for lunch parking. Vast Contact
attractions.nearby: Myriad Botanical Gardens, Paycom Center, Oklahoma City National Memorial & Museum, Scissortail Park, and the Chickasaw Bricktown Ballpark. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Western Avenue District
attractions.description: Western Avenue is one of Oklahoma City’s oldest official districts, featuring a vibrant mix of over 50 local shops, restaurants, bars, and coffee shops. Stretching from Art Deco-inspired buildings to upscale modern developments, it offers a walkable corridor rich in history and local charm. Visit Western Avenue
attractions.address: NW 36th St to Wilshire Blvd along Western Ave
attractions.hours: District: 24/7; Business hours typically 10:00 AM - 10:00 PM (varies by establishment)
attractions.days_open: Daily
attractions.admission: Free (District access); Special event tickets vary
highlights: Over 50 local shops, restaurants, and bars
Historic Route 66 alignment (1931 Beltline)
Vibrant public art and Art Deco architecture
Home to the annual 'Taste of Western' food event
Iconic local businesses like Guestroom Records and Sushi Neko
attractions.tips: The district is very walkable; wear comfortable shoes to explore the murals and boutiques. Parking is generally available on side streets and in business lots. Visit Western Avenue
attractions.nearby: Classen Curve, Nichols Hills Plaza, Penn Square Mall, Oklahoma City University, Asian District

attractions.name: Wheeler District
attractions.description: The Wheeler District is a vibrant, walkable community situated on the south bank of the Oklahoma River in Oklahoma City. Built on the site of a former downtown airpark, the neighborhood features a mix of residential housing, local retail, and a riverfront plaza anchored by the historic Santa Monica Pier Ferris wheel. Wheeler District, VisitOKC
attractions.address: 1801 Wheeler Street, Suite 150, Oklahoma City, OK 73108 (District Office); 1701 S. Western Avenue, Oklahoma City, OK 73109 (Ferris Wheel)
attractions.hours: Monday–Wednesday: CLOSED; Thursday: 12 PM–10 PM; Friday: 10 AM–10 PM; Saturday: 10 AM–10 PM; Sunday: 10 AM–10 PM Wheeler District
attractions.days_open: Thursday–Sunday (Seasonal: Typically April–November) Wheeler District
attractions.admission: Single Ride Ticket: $8, Day Pass: $12, Season Pass: $25, Kids 3 & Under: Free. Access to the riverfront plaza area is free. Wheeler District
highlights: Historic 100-foot Santa Monica Pier Ferris Wheel
Giant 'OKC' letters for photo opportunities
Wheeler Riverfront Plaza with hammocks and yard games
Direct access to the Oklahoma River Trail network for biking and walking
Local dining and retail options including Taco Nation and Clarity Coffee
Seasonal events such as the Night Market and Farmers Market
attractions.tips: The Ferris wheel requires at least two riders per gondola (no single riders). Be sure to take a photo with the giant OKC letters. The district is highly bike-friendly, so consider visiting via the Oklahoma River Trail. Wheeler District, A Better Life OKC
attractions.nearby: Oklahoma River Trails, Scissortail Park, Myriad Botanical Gardens, Downtown Oklahoma City Adventures of A+K

attractions.name: Will Rogers Gardens
attractions.description: Will Rogers Gardens is a historic 30-acre horticultural showplace featuring specialty gardens, a Victorian-style conservatory, and a 10-acre arboretum. Established in the 1930s through collaboration with the Civilian Conservation Corps, it serves as a serene public space and an educational hub for gardening and nature classes (City of Oklahoma City, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 3400 NW 36th St, Oklahoma City, OK 73112-6743
attractions.hours: Garden & Arboretum: 8 a.m. - 8 p.m. (Apr 1 - Sept 30), 8 a.m. - 5 p.m. (Oct 1 - Mar 31) daily; Exhibition Center & Conservatory: 8 a.m. - 5 p.m. weekdays (City of Oklahoma City).
attractions.days_open: Daily (City of Oklahoma City)
attractions.admission: Free (City of Oklahoma City, Wikipedia)
highlights: Ed Lycan Conservatory: A historic glass-enclosed Lord & Burnham greenhouse inspired by Victorian architecture (City of Oklahoma City).
Charles E. Sparks Color Garden: A 2-acre garden featuring seasonal blossoms and a bronze 'Mother and Daughter' fountain (City of Oklahoma City).
Margaret Annis Boys Arboretum: A 10-acre space with hundreds of tree varieties, including native and rare species (City of Oklahoma City).
Will Rogers Garden Exhibition Center: A mid-century building used for garden club meetings and community events (City of Oklahoma City).
Art Installations: Features sculptures such as 'Seed,' 'Wind Harp,' and a bust of Will Rogers (City of Oklahoma City).
Specialty Beds: Extensive collections of peonies, irises, daylilies, and a xeriscape garden (Friends of Will Rogers Gardens).
attractions.tips: Visit in late May for the annual gardening festival; note that indoor facilities like the conservatory have shorter weekday hours than the outdoor park; pets are welcome but must be leashed; smoking is prohibited (City of Oklahoma City, Oklahoma Legal Group).
attractions.nearby: OKC Tennis Center, Will Rogers Senior Center, Will Rogers Park Family Aquatic Center, Decades Revisited Vintage Mall, and Bistro38 Thai (Tripadvisor, City of Oklahoma City).
